name: Cookie Jar Gingersnaps
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a medium bowl, sift together flour, ground ginger, baking soda, cinnamon, cloves, and salt.
3. In a large bowl with an electric mixer, beat the shortening until soft (about 1 minute).
4. Gradually add sugar and continue beating until the mixture is light and fluffy.
5. Beat in egg and molasses until well-blended.
6. Stir in the flour mixture until completely mixed.
7. Place a little sugar in a medium bowl. Scoop out heaping teaspoonfuls of dough and roll into 3/4-inch balls, then drop them into the sugar to coat completely.
8. Place the coated balls 2 inches apart on ungreased baking sheets.
9. Bake until cookies are slightly rounded and tops appear lightly browned and cracked.
10. Remove baking sheets to wire racks to cool slightly.
11. Using a metal pancake turner or palette knife, transfer cookies to wire racks to cool completely.
12. Repeat with remaining dough and sugar.
13. Store in airtight containers.
Note: The original recipe provided does not include specific measurements for the ingredients and instructions, so I have used common baking practices to fill in these details.
